context: Tests whether the agent correctly implements Amazon SP-API authentication using LWA OAuth with token caching, AWS Signature Version 4 signing with the right packages, the modern Listings Items API, and correct inventory update patterns.

checklist.name: LWA token refresh endpoint
checklist.description: Uses https://api.amazon.com/auth/o2/token with grant_type=refresh_token for token retrieval

checklist.name: Token cache with 60s buffer
checklist.description: Caches the access token and checks expiry with at least a 60-second buffer before expiration (e.g., Date.now() + 60000)

checklist.name: SigV4 package: @smithy/signature-v4
checklist.description: Imports SignatureV4 from '@smithy/signature-v4' (not aws4, aws-sdk, or another signing library)

checklist.name: SHA256 package: @aws-crypto/sha256-js
checklist.description: Imports Sha256 from '@aws-crypto/sha256-js' (not a different crypto library)

checklist.name: Listings Items API version
checklist.description: Uses the /listings/2021-08-01/items/ path (not an older Marketplace or MWS endpoint)

checklist.name: PUT for listing create/update
checklist.description: Uses HTTP PUT method when creating or replacing a listing via the Listings Items API

checklist.name: PATCH for inventory update
checklist.description: Uses HTTP PATCH method when updating inventory quantity on an existing listing

checklist.name: fulfillment_availability patch path
checklist.description: Inventory PATCH targets the /attributes/fulfillment_availability path with op='replace'

checklist.name: fulfillment_channel_code DEFAULT
checklist.description: Inventory update includes fulfillment_channel_code: 'DEFAULT' in the patch value

checklist.name: Listing suppression check
checklist.description: Code or notes address checking for listing suppressions in the API response when a listing may be inactive despite a successful submission
